content.text: Microsoft Entra ID is available in four licensing tiers that determine which security, governance, and identity features are available to users in a tenant. Choosing the right tier upfront avoids mid-project redesigns: features like Conditional Access (P1), Privileged Identity Management (P2), and Lifecycle Workflows (Governance) require specific licenses before they can be enabled.

attrs.alt: Entra ID edition tiers: Free, P1, P2, and ID Governance feature comparison

content.text: The Four Tiers

Entra ID licensing is per-user, not per-tenant. A user must have an eligible license assigned to use a licensed feature — the feature is not available to unlicensed users even if the tenant has some P2 seats.
Entra ID Free

Included at no additional cost with any Microsoft cloud subscription (Azure, Microsoft 365, Dynamics). Provides the core directory, basic SSO for up to 10 pre-integrated SaaS apps per tenant, Security Defaults (which enforces per-user MFA registration), B2B guest invitations, and cloud-only self-service password reset.
User and group management, device registration
SSO to pre-integrated SaaS apps (gallery limit applies at tenant level)
Security Defaults: tenant-wide MFA requirement with no per-user or per-app control
B2B guest invitations — guests consume free tier features unless assigned a paid license
Microsoft Entra Connect for hybrid sync (the agent itself is free)
Entra ID P1

P1 adds the features that most enterprises need for a secure baseline. Conditional Access is the headline: fine-grained policies that evaluate user, device, location, and app signals at every authentication. P1 is included in Microsoft 365 E3, Microsoft 365 Business Premium, and EMS E3.
Full Conditional Access policies (location, device compliance, sign-in risk)
Dynamic security groups (membership rule engine based on user attributes)
SSPR with on-premises password writeback via Entra Connect
Hybrid joined device support and seamless SSO for domain-joined machines
Custom directory roles (limited role definitions beyond built-in roles)
Entitlement Management — basic access packages for self-service group membership
Unlimited app integration (no 10-app SSO limit)
Entra ID P2

P2 adds identity governance and risk-based intelligence. The two headline features are Privileged Identity Management (just-in-time role activation) and Identity Protection (ML-driven risk scoring for users and sign-ins). P2 is included in Microsoft 365 E5 and EMS E5.
Privileged Identity Management (PIM) — eligible role assignments, approval workflows, activation time windows
Identity Protection — automated risk detection (leaked credentials, atypical travel, token replay)
Risk-based Conditional Access — dynamically require step-up auth when sign-in risk is medium/high
Access Reviews — periodic reviewer-driven recertification of group memberships and app assignments
Entitlement Management (full) — approval workflows, access packages with expiry, connected organisations
Terms of Use — require users to accept legal terms before accessing applications
Microsoft Entra ID Governance

Identity Governance is a separate add-on SKU (or bundled with Microsoft 365 E5 + Compliance). It extends P2 with HR-driven provisioning automation, Lifecycle Workflows, and advanced ML-powered access insights. This tier targets large enterprises that need joiner/mover/leaver automation.
Lifecycle Workflows — automated tasks on joiner (onboard), mover (transfer), leaver (offboard) events
HR-driven provisioning — Workday or SuccessFactors as identity source of truth
Advanced Access Reviews with AI recommendations and multi-stage reviews
Privileged Access Groups — PIM-enabled group membership for fine-grained JIT access
Custom logic extensions — trigger Logic Apps or Azure Functions from governance workflows
Microsoft Entra Verified ID (decentralised verifiable credentials, full integration)
License Assignment

Licenses are assigned to users (or groups) in the tenant. Group-based licensing (P1 required) is the recommended approach for organisations with more than a few dozen users — it removes the need to manually track individual assignments.
Checking License Assignment via Microsoft Graph
Group-Based License Assignment

import requests

# Assign Entra ID P1 to all members of a group
GROUP_ID = "<group-object-id>"
P1_SKU_ID = "078d2b04-f1bd-4111-bbd4-b4b1b354cef4"  # AAD_PREMIUM

body = {
    "addLicenses": [{"skuId": P1_SKU_ID, "disabledPlans": []}],
    "removeLicenses": []
}

resp = requests.post(
    f"https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/groups/{GROUP_ID}/assignLicense",
    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"},
    json=body
)
print(resp.status_code)  # 202 Accepted — asynchronous assignment

# PowerShell: group-based license assignment
$groupId = "<group-id>"
$p1SkuId = "078d2b04-f1bd-4111-bbd4-b4b1b354cef4"

Set-MgGroupLicense -GroupId $groupId -AddLicenses @{SkuId = $p1SkuId} -RemoveLicenses @()

# Check for license assignment errors (e.g. insufficient seats)
Get-MgGroupMemberWithLicenseError -GroupId $groupId | 
    Select-Object DisplayName, UserPrincipalName, @{n="Error";e={$_.LicenseAssignmentStates.Error}}
Feature Gate: Conditional Access Requires P1

The most common licensing mistake is designing an architecture around Conditional Access for an organisation that has only Entra ID Free licenses (Security Defaults). Security Defaults and Conditional Access are mutually exclusive — you cannot run both simultaneously.
Scenario
Security Defaults (Free)
Conditional Access (P1+)
MFA scope
All users, all apps
Configurable per user/group/app/location
Exclusions
None — no way to exclude break-glass accounts
Fully configurable exclusions
Device compliance
Not evaluated
Require Intune-compliant device
Legacy auth blocking
Automatically blocked
Configurable — block or allow specific protocols
Sign-in risk policies
Not available
Available with P2 (Identity Protection)
Report-only mode
Not available
Built-in — test policy impact before enforcement
If your org has Microsoft 365 Business Premium, you already have P1 — enable Conditional Access and disable Security Defaults to gain per-policy control.
Switching from Security Defaults to Conditional Access
PIM: Just-In-Time Privileged Access (P2)

Privileged Identity Management eliminates standing privileged access. Instead of assigning a user the Global Administrator role permanently, PIM makes them eligible — they can activate the role for a time-limited window when needed, with optional MFA re-authentication and manager approval.
Activating a PIM Role via Microsoft Graph

import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

# A user self-activates an eligible role
# (requires the user to have P2 and be eligible for the role via PIM)

activation = {
    "action": "selfActivate",
    "principalId": "<user-object-id>",
    "roleDefinitionId": "<role-def-id>",   # e.g. Global Administrator role def ID
    "directoryScopeId": "/",               # tenant scope
    "justification": "Emergency RBAC fix for production outage",
    "scheduleInfo": {
        "startDateTime": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "expiration": {
            "type": "afterDuration",
            "duration": "PT4H"             # ISO 8601 duration — 4 hours
        }
    }
}

resp = requests.post(
    "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/roleManagement/directory/roleAssignmentScheduleRequests",
    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}", "Content-Type": "application/json"},
    json=activation
)
print(resp.status_code, resp.json().get("status"))  # 201 Created, status: Granted

# PowerShell: list all PIM-eligible role assignments in the tenant
Get-MgRoleManagementDirectoryRoleEligibilitySchedule -All |
    Select-Object @{n="Principal";e={(Get-MgUser -UserId $_.PrincipalId).UserPrincipalName}},
                  @{n="Role";e={(Get-MgRoleManagementDirectoryRoleDefinition -UnifiedRoleDefinitionId $_.RoleDefinitionId).DisplayName}},
                  @{n="Expiry";e={$_.ScheduleInfo.Expiration.EndDateTime}} |
    Sort-Object Role
License Consumption Report
Tracking how many licenses are consumed versus available prevents over-spending and compliance violations. The following script produces a summary across all active SKUs in the tenant.

import requests

def license_report(token: str) -> None:
    skus = requests.get(
        "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/subscribedSkus",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    ).json().get("value", [])

    # Map SKU IDs to friendly names for key Entra licenses
    KNOWN = {
        "078d2b04-f1bd-4111-bbd4-b4b1b354cef4": "Entra ID P1",
        "84a661c4-e949-4bd2-a560-ed7766fcaf2b": "Entra ID P2",
        "b05e124f-c7cc-45a0-a6aa-8cf78c946968": "EMS E5",
        "06ebc4ee-1bb5-47dd-8120-11324bc54e06": "Microsoft 365 E5",
    }

    print(f"{'SKU':<40} {'Assigned':>8} {'Available':>10} {'Utilisation':>12}")
    print("-" * 74)
    for s in sorted(skus, key=lambda x: x["consumedUnits"], reverse=True):
        name    = KNOWN.get(s["skuId"], s["skuPartNumber"])
        enabled = s["prepaidUnits"]["enabled"]
        used    = s["consumedUnits"]
        util    = f"{used/enabled*100:.1f}%" if enabled > 0 else "N/A"
        print(f"{name:<40} {used:>8} {enabled:>10} {util:>12}")

license_report(access_token)
# SKU                                      Assigned  Available  Utilisation
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# Microsoft 365 E5                             1200       1500        80.0%
# Entra ID P2                                   50        100        50.0%
# Entra ID P1                                  800       1000        80.0%
Common Licensing Mistakes
Mistake
Impact
Fix
Building CA policies with only Free licenses
Policies cannot be created; Security Defaults is the only option
Assign P1 or use M365 Business Premium / E3 which includes P1
Assigning P2 to non-admin users unnecessarily
Cost waste; PIM/Identity Protection are primarily for privileged roles
Audit PIM-eligible assignments; P2 is needed only by the eligible user, not target resources
Not assigning licenses before enabling features
Feature silently unavailable or error at runtime
Assign license, wait for propagation (~15 min), then enable feature
Confusing tenant-level and per-user features
CA policies exist at tenant level but only enforce for licensed users
Test CA policy with a P1-licensed test account before rolling out
Forgetting guest user licensing
Guest users using P1/P2 features without a license violates terms
Guests use your P1/P2 features at a ratio of 1:5 (5 free guests per paid user) under MAU billing
Access Reviews: Recertify Who Has What (P2)

Access Reviews solve the governance problem of entitlement creep — users accumulate group memberships and app assignments over time but are never de-provisioned when their role changes. P2 Access Reviews let you schedule periodic reviewer-driven recertification campaigns.
Creating an Access Review via Microsoft Graph
Retrieving Review Decisions

# After the review period ends, read the decisions
REVIEW_ID = "<access-review-id>"
INSTANCE_ID = "<review-instance-id>"  # one instance per recurrence

decisions = requests.get(
    f"https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identityGovernance/accessReviews/definitions/{REVIEW_ID}/instances/{INSTANCE_ID}/decisions",
    headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {access_token}"}
).json().get("value", [])

for d in decisions:
    print(f"{d['principal']['displayName']:<30} Decision: {d['decision']:<10} Justification: {d.get('justification','')}")

# Decision values: Approve | Deny | DontKnow | NotReviewed | NotApplicable
denied = [d for d in decisions if d["decision"] == "Deny"]
print(f"\n{len(denied)} users denied access"  )
Identity Protection Risk Policies (P2)

Entra ID Identity Protection uses Microsoft's threat intelligence (processing billions of signals daily) to compute risk scores for users and individual sign-in events. These scores feed into risk-based Conditional Access policies that automatically step up authentication when something looks suspicious.
Risk Score Signals
Signal Type
Examples
Risk Level
Leaked credentials
Username/password found in breach database
High
Atypical travel
Sign-in from Lagos 30 min after sign-in from London
Medium–High
Malware-linked IP
Sign-in from IP associated with botnet C2
High
Anonymous IP
Sign-in via Tor or known VPN exit node
Medium
Unfamiliar sign-in properties
New device, OS, browser not seen before
Low–Medium
Password spray
Slow brute-force across many accounts
High
Token replay
Stolen refresh token used from different IP
High
Risk-Based Conditional Access Policy
Lifecycle Workflows: Joiner/Mover/Leaver Automation (ID Governance)

Entra ID Governance Lifecycle Workflows automate identity tasks that fire when a user attribute changes — hire date, department change, last day. This replaces the manual HR-to-IT ticket workflow with event-driven automation.
Built-In Task Types
Workflow Category
Example Task
Trigger
Joiner
Generate temporary access pass
employeeHireDate - 7 days
Joiner
Add user to onboarding group
employeeHireDate
Joiner
Send welcome email with credentials
employeeHireDate
Mover
Add to new department group
department attribute change
Mover
Remove from old team group
department attribute change
Leaver
Disable account
employeeLeaveDateTime - 0 days
Leaver
Remove all group memberships
employeeLeaveDateTime
Leaver
Revoke all active sessions and tokens
employeeLeaveDateTime
Creating a Leaver Workflow via Microsoft Graph
Choosing the Right License Tier: Decision Guide
Use the following decision questions to determine which license tier fits a given requirement:
Do you need per-policy MFA control (exclude break-glass, per-app, per-location)? → P1 minimum (Conditional Access)
Do you need to block legacy authentication selectively while allowing it for specific apps? → P1 (CA client app conditions)
Do your admins hold permanent privileged role assignments? → P2 for PIM just-in-time activation
Do you need automated response to credential compromise (leaked passwords, atypical travel)? → P2 for Identity Protection
Do you need periodic reviewer-driven recertification of group memberships? → P2 for Access Reviews
Do you need to automate onboarding/offboarding with attribute-driven triggers? → ID Governance (Lifecycle Workflows)
Do you source identity from Workday or SuccessFactors as the system of record? → ID Governance (HR-driven provisioning)

If you are unsure, start with P1. It covers 80% of enterprise security requirements. Upgrade specific users to P2 when PIM or Identity Protection become a requirement — you do not need to upgrade all users simultaneously.
Summary

Entra ID licensing determines which security and governance features are available per user. The four tiers are Free (directory + basic SSO + Security Defaults), P1 (Conditional Access, dynamic groups, SSPR writeback), P2 (PIM, Identity Protection, Access Reviews), and ID Governance (Lifecycle Workflows, HR provisioning, advanced reviews).
P1 is the baseline for any enterprise deploying Conditional Access — included in M365 E3/Business Premium
P2 adds PIM (just-in-time privileged access) and Identity Protection (risk-based policies) — included in M365 E5
Identity Governance adds joiner/mover/leaver automation via Lifecycle Workflows
Licensing is per-user: assign licenses before enabling features; use group-based licensing at scale
Security Defaults and Conditional Access are mutually exclusive — disable Security Defaults when moving to CA
Guest users consume licensed features under a 1:5 MAU model for B2B collaboration scenarios
